name: input-disabled
visual_summary: The input-disabled component is a visual representation of a disabled input field, often displayed in a grayed out state with a faint text. It is typically used to provide visual feedback to users when a form element cannot be interacted with. A developer would use this component when creating forms or registration pages with input fields that are disabled by either a system or by a certain set of conditions.
interaction_tags: e.g., does not react to onClick events
e.g. does not change on hover
generation_prompt:
Create a production-ready input component with the following characteristics:

Name: input-disabled
Description: The input-disabled component is a visual representation of a disabled input field, often displayed in a grayed out state with a faint text. It is typically used to provide visual feedback to users when a form element cannot be interacted with. A developer would use this component when creating forms or registration pages with input fields that are disabled by either a system or by a certain set of conditions.
Style: e.g. material, e.g. ios, e.g. dark
Interactions: e.g., does not react to onClick events, e.g. does not change on hover
Tags: disabled, input, form, stateful

Requirements:
- Works in React with TypeScript by default but adaptable to Vue, Svelte, or plain HTML/CSS
- Uses Tailwind CSS for styling
- Fully accessible with ARIA labels and keyboard navigation
- Responsive and mobile-friendly
- No external dependencies beyond React and Tailwind unless necessary
- Include all necessary imports
- Export as default with sensible prop types

Generate clean production-ready code only. No explanations.
